title: Alison Minard
content:
People.ContactEmail aminard@mccarthy.ca
- University of Ottawa
Known for her highly personable and practical approach, Alison offers creative and tax-efficient advice on a wide range of trust and estate matters
Alison Minard is a partner in our National Tax Group in Toronto. Her practice encompasses all areas of trust and estate planning and administration.
With a proven ability to advise individual and corporate clients on a wide range of Canadian and international trust and estate planning matters, Alison provides thoughtful and creative advice to clients. She prepares sophisticated estate planning documents such as domestic and cross-border wills, trusts and powers of attorney.
Alison also advises clients on a variety of issues pertaining to trust and estate administration and post-mortem planning. She acts for Canadian and foreign trustees, executors, estate beneficiaries, and attorneys for property and personal care on a variety of contentious and non-contentious issues. She also assists with will and trust interpretations, trust variations, and the passing of fiduciary accounts.
Having advised financial institutions in a previous role on issues pertaining to the administration of estate assets, registered and non-registered accounts, and issues relating to powers of attorney and capacity matters, Alison brings the skills and experience acquired during that period to her estate and trust practice.
Alison is a frequent writer and speaker on a variety of topics relating to trust and estate planning and estate administration, and has written and co-authored articles and chapters for Estates Trusts & Pensions Journal, the Ontario Bar Association, and the Financial & Estate Planning for the Mature Client in Ontario (Butterworths).
Alison earned her LLB (Cum Laude) in 2008 from the University of Ottawa. Prior to this, she earned a BA (with Distinction) from Dalhousie University. Alison was called to the Ontario Bar in 2009 and is a member of the Law Society of Ontario, the Canadian Bar Association, the Ontario Bar Association and the Society of Trust and Estate Practitioners (STEP).
